cards.name: Vegan Bistro Donuts & Cafe
cards.address: 449 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a donut and coffee from this highly loved vegan cafe on Santa Clara St — the donuts are the real draw, so grab a couple and take your time.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so hit it early before the good stuff sells out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition on your way through Santa Clara — it's a visually interesting show worth at least an hour of your afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, and daytime-only so now is the time to catch it.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and just pick whatever looks good — tacos, banh mi, ramen, it's all under one roof and you can grab a patio seat if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: Best all-in-one lunch spot downtown with no wrong answer on what to order.

cards.name: SJSU Opera Theatre presents a Double Bill: La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator at Hammer Theatre Center
cards.blurb: Head to Hammer Theatre for the SJSU Opera Theatre double bill — La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator are two very different pieces and together they make for a genuinely interesting afternoon of live performance.
cards.why: It's free and it's live opera in a great venue, which is a rare Sunday afternoon treat.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge, where you pick a piece, paint it however you want, and they fire it for you — genuinely fun for adults and a great low-key way to end the afternoon.
cards.why: It's creative, relaxed, and a solid way to decompress after a full day out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Wander through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU — the kind of visual art show that messes with your perception in the best way.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely interesting way to spend a cool morning.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library and check out Fists of Fury, an exhibition digging into the history and culture of martial arts in America.
cards.why: Free, fascinating, and the kind of thing you'd never stumble on otherwise.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and mix and match from a dozen vendors — tacos, ramen, banh mi, whatever sounds good. Snag a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: It's the most fun way to do lunch downtown when you can't agree on one thing.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: Bowl a few frames at the SJSU Student Union Bowling Center. It's a real lanes setup, not a pop-up, and it's open to the public.
cards.why: Low-key competitive and way more fun than it sounds on a Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row in the early evening, browse the boutiques, and find a table at one of the outdoor restaurants when you're ready to sit down.
cards.why: The boulevard is relaxed on a weekday evening and the patio vibes are solid.

cards.name: LUNA Mexican Kitchen - San Jose
cards.address: 1495 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with dinner at LUNA Mexican Kitchen on The Alameda. Order margaritas and something off the dinner menu and call it a good Monday.
cards.why: Solid Mexican food with a vibe that doesn't feel rushed.

cards.name: Is a Liveable Planet a Human Right? The International Court of Justice weighs in
cards.blurb: Catch this talk on whether a liveable planet qualifies as a human right, with the International Court of Justice weighing in — it's the kind of session that actually gives you something to argue about over lunch.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and it kicks off your day with something genuinely interesting.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Computer History Museum and let yourself get lost in the full arc of computing history, from room-sized machines to the pocket computers we ignore at dinner.
cards.why: You don't have to be a tech person to find this stuff genuinely wild.

cards.name: Next Level Board Game Cafe
cards.address: 888 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Next Level Board Game Cafe for lunch and pick a game off their massive shelf while you eat — it's a full sit-down experience, not just a cafe with a shelf of Settlers in the corner.
cards.why: A Sunday afternoon with board games and good food is basically unbeatable.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Stevens Creek Trail and let the afternoon light do its thing — flat, easy, and scenic enough that you'll actually enjoy the walk instead of just surviving it.
cards.why: 75 degrees and sunny on a trail with no agenda is exactly what Sunday was invented for.

cards.name: tUrn Climate Science en Español: Glaciares y Deshielo Glaciar | Glaciars & Glacial Melt
cards.blurb: Catch the Climate Science talk on glaciers and glacial melt, presented in both English and Spanish — it's a tight, focused session that doesn't require a science background to follow.
cards.why: Free afternoon talk with real substance, and it sets up a good conversation over dinner.

cards.name: The Stand - American Classics Redefined
cards.address: 785 Lawrence Expy, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Grab dinner at The Stand on Lawrence Expressway, where the menu leans into elevated American classics done right — burgers, fries, the works.
cards.why: Solid food without the Sunday evening wait you'd get at a trendier spot.

cards.name: tUrn Director’s Keynote: Why do we have catastrophe apathy?
cards.blurb: End the night at the Director's Keynote asking why humans are so bad at caring about catastrophe — it's provocative, free, and a genuinely great way to close out a day that started with climate science.
cards.why: Wraps the whole day into a satisfying arc and gives you something to actually think about on the drive home.

item.title: From Bestseller to Classic: What Makes a Book Endure?
item.name: From Bestseller to Classic: What Makes a Book Endure?
item.summary: Why do some books become classics? Two book buffs join us to explore why certain novels stand the test of time. Elizabeth Rosner is joined in lively conversation with veteran critic John McMurtrie (formerly of the San Francisco Chronicle ) about what makes certain novels resonate with readers over time.

copy.x: Tonight at Kepler's in Palo Alto: why do some books become classics while others fade? Elizabeth Rosner and critic John McMurtrie dig into that question at 6 PM. Worth the drive. https://www.keplers.org/upcoming-events-internal/classic-books
copy.threads: Tonight at Kepler's in Palo Alto, two people who've spent their lives thinking about books sit down to ask the big question: why do some novels last forever while others disappear? Elizabeth Rosner and veteran critic John McMurtrie (formerly of the SF Chronicle) get into it at 6 PM. Free, smart, and the kind of conversation you'll keep having on the drive home. 📚 https://www.keplers.org/upcoming-events-internal/classic-books #PaloAlto #BookLovers #SouthBay
copy.bluesky: Tonight at Kepler's in Palo Alto — Elizabeth Rosner and SF Chronicle critic John McMurtrie tackle the question: what actually makes a book a classic? 6 PM, and it's the kind of talk that'll rearrange your reading list. #BookLovers #PaloAlto
copy.facebook: If you love books and have ever wondered why certain novels stick around for centuries while others vanish, tonight at Kepler's in Palo Alto is worth your evening. Author Elizabeth Rosner sits down with veteran critic John McMurtrie — formerly of the San Francisco Chronicle — to dig into exactly that question. It starts at 6 PM and sounds like the kind of conversation that's genuinely hard to leave. https://www.keplers.org/upcoming-events-internal/classic-books
copy.instagram:
Tonight's pick for the South Bay 📚

Kepler's Books in Palo Alto is hosting one of those conversations that sounds simple on the surface — why do some books become classics? — but gets surprisingly deep once you're in the room.

Author Elizabeth Rosner is joined by John McMurtrie, a veteran literary critic who spent years at the San Francisco Chronicle, and together they're going to pull apart what makes certain novels resonate with readers across generations while others quietly disappear.

It starts at 6 PM. If you've ever finished a book and thought "this one's going to matter for a long time" — or picked up a so-called classic and wondered why anyone bothered — this is your crowd.

Kepler's has been running conversations like this for decades, and they tend to feel more like a great dinner party discussion than a lecture. Come with opinions.

cards.blurb: Start the morning at Café San José, a cozy neighborhood spot open early. Grab coffee and breakfast before the day kicks off.
cards.why: It opens at 7 and closes at 2, so hit it early while it's still fresh and calm.

cards.name: Fremont Older Open Space
cards.address: Fremont Older Open Space Preserve
cards.blurb: Lace up and get into the Fremont Older Open Space Preserve. The foothill trails open up to sweeping views of the valley and barely anyone is out here on a Sunday morning.
cards.why: Perfect temperature for a hike and the views at the top are genuinely worth it.

cards.name: Rio Adobe Southwest Cafe
cards.address: 10525 S De Anza Blvd #100, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Rio Adobe Southwest Cafe is a casual Cupertino spot for solid Southwestern-style food. Order the burritos, grab a patio seat if the weather holds, and decompress after the hike.
cards.why: Affordable, unpretentious, and the kind of place that just hits right after a morning outside.

cards.name: Spring Wine Walk
cards.blurb: Head over to Los Gatos for the Spring Wine Walk. You stroll through downtown, pop into shops and tasting spots, and sip your way through the afternoon.
cards.why: A gorgeous 74-degree Sunday afternoon in Los Gatos is basically the platonic ideal setting for a wine walk.

cards.name: Almaden Lake Park
cards.address: 6099 Winfield Blvd, San Jose, CA 95120, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Almaden Lake Park and see what's going on.
cards.why: A real beach in the South Bay — free swimming in summer, relaxed walking year-round

cards.name: Benihana - Cupertino
cards.address: Fashion Park, 2074 Vallco Pkwy, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Top-rated food spot in cupertino.

cards.name: Rose Café & Donuts
cards.address: 994 Saratoga Ave, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Rose Café & Donuts — grab a coffee and a fresh donut before the day gets going.
cards.why: A low-key local spot that's way better than a chain for your first stop of the day.

cards.name: OMESCAPE | Sunnyvale
cards.address: 1135 E Arques Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Book a room at OMESCAPE and work through one of their escape room challenges with your crew — they're genuinely well-designed and the rating speaks for itself.
cards.why: Perfect indoor activity on a mellow Sunday morning before things get crowded.

cards.name: Opelia
cards.address: 25 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Opelia for lunch — it's a solid sit-down spot in downtown Los Gatos with a tight window, so go early and settle in on the patio if they've got space.
cards.why: Los Gatos on a sunny Sunday is exactly where you want to be for a leisurely lunch.

cards.name: St. Joseph's Hill Open Space Preserve
cards.address: Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Burn off lunch on the trails at St. Joseph's Hill Open Space Preserve — the views over the Los Gatos hills are worth every step and the upper loop gives you a real sense of the landscape.
cards.why: 75 degrees and sunny with trails right in Los Gatos is a hard combo to beat.

cards.name: Heroes & Champions
cards.address: 574 E El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into Heroes & Champions in Sunnyvale and dig through the comics, collectibles, and gaming gear — it's the kind of shop you don't rush through.
cards.why: Great way to wind down the afternoon before dinner without committing to anything too serious.

cards.name: Salt & Straw
cards.address: 2855 Stevens Creek Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Salt & Straw for a scoop or two before dinner — their rotating seasonal flavors are always interesting and the lines move fast on a Sunday evening.
cards.why: It's a genuinely good ice cream break and the Stevens Creek location is easy to pop into.

cards.name: Fox Hole Golf Club
cards.address: 545 Aldo Ave #13, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Cap the night with a round at Fox Hole Golf Club, a bar and mini golf setup in Santa Clara that's perfect for a Sunday evening with drinks in hand.
cards.why: It's fun, it's low-stakes, and it's a much better Sunday night than sitting on a couch.

item.title: Annie Leonard and André Carothers on Protest
item.name: Annie Leonard and André Carothers on Protest
item.summary: What would the world look like if we couldn’t express outrage against the systems we disagree with or support the changes we seek? In Protest! Respect It Defend It Use It, lifelong activists Annie Leonard and André Carothers celebrate what collective action can achieve, and invite each of us to make a difference.
